analysis: The heuristics fall into two broad categories. Some rely on aggressive candidate pruning (skyline filters and domination tests) to reduce overhead, while others use Monte Carlo sampling with incrementally updated coverage counts to estimate marginal contributions. Efficient ones combine fast approximate evaluations with a refined local swap phase using exact hypervolume calculations (via pygmo) only on a small candidate shortlist. They reuse sampling data with chunking to manage memory and avoid repeated computations, whereas weaker variants compute expensive hypervolume contributions repeatedly.
experience: Focus on aggressive pre-pruning and reuse shared samples with vectorized, chunked computations. Incorporate a two-stage approach: fast greedy selection with approximate estimates followed by a brief, exact local refinement on a shortlist to improve quality without incurring excessive overhead.
current_reflection: Focus on aggressive pre-pruning and reuse shared samples with vectorized, chunked computations. Incorporate a two-stage approach: fast greedy selection with approximate estimates followed by a brief, exact local refinement on a shortlist to improve quality without incurring excessive overhead.
bad_reflections: Focus on reducing candidate size via pruning, maximize vectorized operations and shared sample reuse, and limit full‐HV recalculations with local swap refinements. Keep memory usage controlled by chunking computations to boost overall runtime efficiency.

Focus on early pruning, vectorized operations, and shared sampling to reduce overhead. Use block-chunking and caching for expensive computations, and integrate small-budget local refinements to polish results efficiently.

Focus on efficient pre‐processing (like skyline pruning) and reuse MC samples with vectorized, incremental updates. Limit expensive exact hypervolume calls by confining local swaps to small candidate shortlists, thus balancing quality and speed.
comprehensive_reflection:
• Keywords: aggressive pre-pruning, vectorized and chunked computations, shared sampling, two-stage selection (approximate followed by local refinement).  
• Advice: Employ a fast greedy selection with rough estimates, then refine a shortlist exactly to boost quality without heavy overhead.  
• Avoid: Repeating full expensive calculations or using unbounded memory; steer clear of prolonged computations on large candidate sets.  
• Explanation: This approach combines rapid initial filtering with targeted refinement, balancing speed and quality by reducing unnecessary computations and capitalizing on efficient vectorized operations.
